reason: Both correctly land on the same core recommendation: contractor now to stabilize/migrate, move to managed K8s (EKS/GKE), avoid re-hiring a single full-time DevOps replacement. Answer A is crisp and directionally correct but thin — it doesn't address the disproportionate 40-services-for-10-engineers load beyond one line, and gives no concrete plan or numbers to back the recommendation. Answer B covers the same conclusion with more decision-relevant substance: it quantifies the cost tradeoff (hire cost vs. managed control-plane premium), explicitly calls out the risk of self-managed ops falling on developers, gives a concrete phased plan (contractor 4-8wk → migrate → later hire a part-time/platform-minded engineer so 10 engineers can share on-call), and warns against a risky full replatform right now. This directly reinforces the bus-factor point and the disproportionate-load point, and stays decision-focused rather than padded. Neither explicitly raises long-term service consolidation, so that's a wash. Given B captures more of the key points with concrete reasoning without meaningful fluff, it's the stronger answer.
